title: Demo scenario: find where the UI settles from motion alone (no speech)
prompt: media/demo.mp4 is a screen recording of an app demo (no narration). For the hero shot we need the moment the intro animation finishes and the UI first settles - motion stops and the frame holds still. Find that time in seconds and write answer.json as {"settle": <seconds>}. Do not render anything. A `ripple` CLI is available on PATH - run `ripple help` first.
setup: ffmpeg -y -v error -f lavfi -i "color=c=0x303030:s=1280x720:d=18:r=30" -f lavfi -i "color=c=white:s=160x160:d=18:r=30" -f lavfi -i "anullsrc=r=48000:cl=stereo" -filter_complex "[0][1]overlay=x='if(lt(t,6),60+(t/6)*1000,1060)':y='if(lt(t,9),280,if(lt(t,14.5),280+((t-9)/5.5)*160,440))'[v]" -map "[v]" -map 2:a -t 18 -c:v libx264 -preset veryfast -pix_fmt yuv420p -c:a aac media/demo.mp4
